title: Serialization

sections.markdown: **Serialization** attaches a unique per-unit identifier to each physical item. GTIN identifies the product type; serialization identifies **this specific bottle / this specific pallet**. The GS1 EPC syntax combines them: **SGTIN** = GTIN + serial, **SSCC** = its own serial.

sections.code:
SGTIN (Serialized GTIN) URN:
  urn:epc:id:sgtin:8901234.00001.SN-777
           GCP           Item     Serial

On a pack (GS1 DataMatrix):
  <FNC1> 01 08901234000015 21 SN-777 17 261231 10 BATCH42
     GTIN(14)          Serial(≤20)   Expiry     Batch

Regulatory 'four elements' on pharma packs (DSCSA, EU FMD):
  GTIN + Serial + Batch + Expiry

sections.markdown: Serial-space design matters: use a **random serial** for tamper resistance (attackers can't guess valid serials) or a **counter** for auditability, but never mix. Keep the space large (10–12 chars) so exhaustion doesn't force reissue. Reserve a checksum/HMAC-like segment if you want offline validation.
Sequential serials 000001, 000002, … printed in the clear. Attackers infer volumes and generate valid-looking clones. Prefer **randomized** or **format-preserving encrypted** serials.

For pharma/regulated flows, generate serials centrally with an authenticated allocator; log every allocation to an EPCIS commissioning event. For consumer engagement, keep serials short + human-friendly on-pack (for shorter URLs).

flashcards.back: The URN combining a GTIN and a per-item serial to identify one specific physical unit.
GTIN + Serial + Batch + Expiry — required by DSCSA / EU FMD.
Attackers infer volumes and mint valid-looking clones. Prefer randomized or FPE serials.

quiz.explain: Non-guessable serials are harder to clone.

challenges.text: Emit an SGTIN URN from a GTIN + serial.
Design a serial allocator that guarantees uniqueness + auditable log.
Emit an EPCIS commissioning event for a batch of 10k serials.
Design a two-stage anti-counterfeit strategy: printed serial + server-signed short token stored in Digital Link params.

interviewQuestions: How would you allocate serials for a pharma line?
How do serials integrate with EPCIS?
